title: If I Were You – Medium SAQs

questions.question: Describe Gerrard’s personality.
questions.answer: Gerrard is calm, intelligent and quick-thinking. Even when threatened by the intruder, he remains confident and uses clever conversation to control the situation.

questions.question: Why does the intruder enter Gerrard’s cottage?
questions.answer: The intruder enters Gerrard’s cottage to kill him and take his identity so that he can escape the police.

questions.question: Why is the intruder being hunted by the police?
questions.answer: The intruder is being hunted because he has committed murder and killed a policeman.

questions.question: Why does the intruder want to assume Gerrard’s identity?
questions.answer: He wants to assume Gerrard’s identity because they look similar and Gerrard lives alone, making it easier for him to hide from the police.

questions.question: How does Gerrard react when the intruder threatens him?
questions.answer: Gerrard stays calm and speaks confidently. Instead of panicking, he uses clever words to confuse the intruder.

questions.question: Why does Gerrard pretend that he is a criminal?
questions.answer: Gerrard pretends to be a criminal in order to confuse the intruder and prevent him from killing him.

questions.question: How does Gerrard gain control of the situation?
questions.answer: Gerrard gains control by speaking calmly and inventing a story that makes the intruder doubt his own plan.

questions.question: Why does the intruder hesitate after hearing Gerrard’s story?
questions.answer: He hesitates because Gerrard claims to be a criminal himself, which makes the intruder uncertain about killing him.

questions.question: What mistake does the intruder make?
questions.answer: The intruder makes the mistake of trusting Gerrard’s story and lowering his guard.

questions.question: How does Gerrard trap the intruder?
questions.answer: Gerrard pushes the intruder into a cupboard and locks it from outside.

questions.question: What happens to the revolver during the struggle?
questions.answer: The revolver falls from the intruder’s hand when Gerrard pushes him into the cupboard.

questions.question: What does Gerrard do after trapping the intruder?
questions.answer: He picks up the revolver and calls the police to arrest the criminal.

questions.question: What kind of atmosphere does the play create?
questions.answer: The play creates a tense and suspenseful atmosphere because the intruder threatens Gerrard’s life.

questions.question: Why does Gerrard speak ironically?
questions.answer: He uses irony to confuse the intruder and hide his fear.

questions.question: How does dialogue help develop the story?
questions.answer: Dialogue reveals the characters’ thoughts and gradually builds suspense.

questions.question: What is the central conflict of the play?
questions.answer: The central conflict is the intruder’s attempt to kill Gerrard and take his identity.

questions.question: What message does the play convey?
questions.answer: The play shows that intelligence and presence of mind can overcome danger.

questions.question: Why is Gerrard considered clever?
questions.answer: He defeats the intruder without using violence and relies on his intelligence.

questions.question: How does the play end?
questions.answer: The intruder is locked in a cupboard and the police are informed.

questions.question: What role does suspense play in the play?
questions.answer: Suspense keeps the audience engaged and highlights the danger faced by Gerrard.

questions.question: What does the intruder represent?
questions.answer: He represents crime and violence in society.

questions.question: What does Gerrard represent?
questions.answer: He represents intelligence, courage and presence of mind.

questions.question: What lesson does Gerrard’s victory teach readers?
questions.answer: It teaches that calm thinking and intelligence can defeat danger.

questions.question: Why is the title 'If I Were You' suitable?
questions.answer: It suggests advice given to someone to act wisely in difficult situations.

questions.question: What quality of Gerrard ultimately saves his life?
questions.answer: His presence of mind and intelligence save his life.
